title: Phase 4: Build & Quality Monitor
description: The Adjutant monitors build/test results, creates bug beads for failures, enforces quality gates on bead closure, and coordinates branch merges to prevent conflicts. Three behaviors: build-monitor, quality-gate, merge-coordinator. Adds build/merge EventBus events. Military parallel: the adjutant inspects the troops and reports irregularities.

title: Add build/merge events to EventBus
description: Add 4 new event types to EventMap: build:failed (BuildFailedEvent with agentId, exitCode, errorOutput, streamId), build:passed (BuildPassedEvent), merge:completed (MergeCompletedEvent with branch, beadId), merge:conflict (MergeConflictEvent with branch, conflictFiles). TDD. Files: backend/src/services/event-bus.ts, backend/tests/unit/event-bus.test.ts
close_reason: Added 4 new event types (build:failed, build:passed, merge:completed, merge:conflict) with interfaces, EventMap entries, SSE type mappings, and listenerCounts tracking. 12 new TDD tests.

title: Build Monitor behavior
description: Create behaviors/build-monitor.ts. Triggers: stream:status. On stream completion with exit code \!= 0: parse error output, create bug bead via bd CLI, assign to idle agent if available or escalate to user. Guards: only known agent sessions, ignore bug-fix bead streams (avoid loops), rate limit 1 bug bead per agent per 10 min. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/build-monitor.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/build-monitor.test.ts
close_reason: Created build-monitor behavior with TDD (15 tests). Triggers on build:failed/build:passed. Rate-limited notifications, bug-fix loop prevention, agent messaging.

title: Quality Gate behavior
description: Create behaviors/quality-gate.ts. Triggers: bead:closed. On task/bug bead closure, verify: build passed, tests passed, branch merged to main. If any check fails, reopen bead with explanation. Skip epics and beads closed with 'by-design' or 'deferred' reason. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/quality-gate.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/quality-gate.test.ts

title: Merge Coordinator behavior
description: Create behaviors/merge-coordinator.ts. Triggers: bead:closed. Track branches ready to merge, serialize merges to main to prevent conflicts. On merge conflict: create resolution bead and assign to original agent. After merge: verify main still builds, emit merge:completed. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/merge-coordinator.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/merge-coordinator.test.ts

title: Integrate Phase 4 behaviors into index.ts
description: Register build-monitor, quality-gate, and merge-coordinator behaviors in index.ts. Verify full integration: build passes, all tests pass. Files: backend/src/index.ts

title: Phase 5: Epic Management
description: The Adjutant plans epics from user intent, auto-closes eligible epics, reports progress proactively at milestones, and generates retrospectives with performance metrics. Four behaviors: epic-planner, epic-closer, progress-reporter, retrospective. Adds epic progress tracking to state store. Military parallel: the adjutant plans the campaign.

title: Epic progress tracking in state store
description: Create migration 012-epic-progress.sql with adjutant_epic_progress table (epic_id PK, total_tasks, completed_tasks, started_at, last_activity, milestone_25/50/75, completed_at). Add methods to state-store.ts: upsertEpicProgress(), getEpicProgress(), getAllActiveEpics(). TDD. Files: backend/src/services/migrations/012-epic-progress.sql, backend/src/services/adjutant/state-store.ts

title: Epic Planner behavior
description: Create behaviors/epic-planner.ts. Triggers: mail:received. Detect user messages with epic intent (keywords like 'build', 'create', 'implement', or /plan prefix). Invoke Adjutant agent via tmux with planning directive. Agent uses epic-planner skill to generate spec artifacts and beads. Report plan summary to user for approval. Guards: only user messages, require intent keywords, no duplicate in-progress epics. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/epic-planner.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/epic-planner.test.ts

title: Epic Closer behavior
description: Create behaviors/epic-closer.ts. Triggers: bead:closed. Schedule: */30 min sweep. On bead closure, check if parent epic is close-eligible (all children closed). If so, close parent automatically via bd CLI, generate completion summary, send announcement. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/epic-closer.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/epic-closer.test.ts

title: Progress Reporter behavior
description: Create behaviors/progress-reporter.ts. Triggers: bead:closed, bead:updated, agent:status_changed. Track epic progress (N of M tasks complete). On milestones (25/50/75/100%), send proactive update to user. Detect stalled epics (no bead closures in 2+ hours) and escalate. Include burndown data in routine queue for hourly summary. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/progress-reporter.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/progress-reporter.test.ts

title: Retrospective behavior
description: Create behaviors/retrospective.ts. Schedule: daily or on epic completion. Gather metrics: beads closed, time per bead, agents used, failures encountered. Identify patterns: fastest agents, longest beads, common failure types. Generate retrospective summary for user. Store patterns in state metadata. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/retrospective.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/retrospective.test.ts

title: Integrate Phase 5 behaviors into index.ts
description: Register epic-planner, epic-closer, progress-reporter, and retrospective behaviors in index.ts. Verify full integration: build passes, all tests pass. Files: backend/src/index.ts

title: Persistent Self-Correcting Memory System
description: Give the Adjutant persistent, self-correcting memory: SQLite-backed learnings, session retrospectives, correction tracking, startup review, self-improvement proposals, and auto-memory file sync. Supersedes adj-052.4.5. Spec: specs/033-persistent-memory/spec.md

title: Phase 1: Memory Store (Foundation)
description: SQLite migration for memory tables (adjutant_learnings, adjutant_retrospectives, adjutant_corrections, FTS5), typed MemoryStore interface with CRUD methods, and new EventBus event types (correction:detected, learning:created). This is the data layer that every other phase depends on.
close_reason: All tasks and QA bugs resolved. Migration, store, EventBus events — 11 bugs fixed.

title: SQLite migration for memory tables
description: Create migration 011-memory-store.sql with tables: adjutant_learnings (category, topic, content, source_type, source_ref, confidence, reinforcement_count, superseded_by), adjutant_retrospectives (session_date, beads_closed/failed, corrections_received, agents_used, went_well/wrong/action_items as JSON), adjutant_corrections (message_id, correction_type, pattern, description, learning_id FK, recurrence_count, resolved). Add FTS5 virtual table adjutant_learnings_fts with insert/update/delete triggers. Add indexes on category, topic, confidence, correction learning_id, retro date. File: backend/src/services/migrations/011-memory-store.sql
close_reason: Migration 011-memory-store.sql created with all 3 tables, FTS5, triggers, and indexes. 23 tests passing.

title: Memory store typed interface + CRUD
description: Create memory-store.ts with typed MemoryStore interface and implementation: insertLearning, getLearning, updateLearning, findSimilarLearnings (FTS), queryLearnings (filter by category/topic/minConfidence), searchLearnings (FTS5 full-text), reinforceLearning (increment count + boost confidence), supersedeLearning, pruneStale. Retrospectives: insertRetrospective, getRecentRetrospectives. Corrections: insertCorrection, findSimilarCorrection, incrementRecurrence, getUnresolvedCorrections. Analytics: getTopicFrequency, getCorrectionRecurrenceRate, getLearningEffectiveness. Follow same pattern as state-store.ts (prepared statements, row mappers). TDD: write tests first. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-store.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/memory-store.test.ts
close_reason: Memory store interface with full CRUD, FTS5 search, reinforcement, supersede, pruning, analytics. 30 tests passing.

title: Add correction:detected and learning:created events to EventBus
description: Add two new event types to EventMap in event-bus.ts: CorrectionDetectedEvent (messageId, from, pattern, body) and LearningCreatedEvent (learningId, category, topic, sourceType). Update EventName type. File: backend/src/services/event-bus.ts

title: Phase 2: Memory Collector (Capture)
description: Behavior that captures learnings from user corrections (keyword heuristic matching on mail:received), bead outcomes (failure patterns from bead:closed), and agent failures. Includes deduplication via FTS similarity and confidence scoring with reinforcement.
close_reason: All tasks and QA bugs resolved. Memory-collector with correction detection — 3 bugs fixed.

title: Create memory-collector behavior with correction detection
description: Create memory-collector behavior implementing AdjutantBehavior interface. Triggers: mail:received, bead:closed, agent:status_changed. On mail:received from user: match body against CORRECTION_PATTERNS regex array (don't/never/stop + verb, always/must/should + verb, remember/note/important, wrong/mistake + should/instead). If match: infer category (operational/technical/coordination/project) from keyword sets, extract topic, create learning via memory store, emit correction:detected and learning:created events. Dedup: before creating, call findSimilarLearnings — if match exists, reinforce instead. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.test.ts
close_reason: Memory-collector behavior with correction detection via regex patterns, dedup, and learning creation. 13 tests passing.

title: Add bead outcome capture to memory-collector
description: Extend memory-collector to capture learnings from bead:closed events. Detect failure patterns: bead was reopened (check decisions log for reopen actions), bead spawned bug children, bead took multiple attempts (same agent assigned/unassigned). On failure pattern: create learning with source_type=bead_outcome, source_ref=beadId. On agent:status_changed to blocked/disconnected while working on bead: log potential learning about task difficulty. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.ts
close_reason: Bead outcome capture: detects reopened and multi-assignment failure patterns in decision log, creates bead_outcome learnings. 5 tests.

title: Deduplication + confidence scoring logic
description: Add deduplication and confidence scoring to memory-store.ts: findSimilarLearnings uses FTS match score with threshold 0.8, reinforceLearning increments reinforcement_count and boosts confidence by 10% (capped at 1.0), add confidence decay method (5% per week without reinforcement), supersedeLearning marks old learning and transfers reinforcement count to new one. Add unit tests for all scoring edge cases. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-store.ts
close_reason: Comprehensive dedup + confidence scoring tests: 21 tests covering FTS5 dedup, confidence boost/cap, supersede transfer, stale decay, correction recurrence.

title: Phase 3: Session Retrospective (Analysis)
description: Daily behavior generating structured retrospectives with metrics (beads closed/failed, corrections, agents used, avg bead time) and reflection (went_well, went_wrong, action_items). Supersedes adj-052.4.5 retrospective behavior.
close_reason: All tasks and QA bugs resolved. Session retrospective — 2 bugs fixed.

title: Create session-retrospective behavior
description: Create session-retrospective behavior implementing AdjutantBehavior. Name: session-retrospective. Triggers: [] (schedule-only). Schedule: '0 23 * * *' (daily 11 PM). shouldAct: always true. act: gather metrics since last retro (check meta key last_retro_at): count beads closed from decisions log, count corrections inserted today from memory store, count distinct active agents, calculate avg bead time. Supersedes adj-052.4.5. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.test.ts

title: Retro analysis + generation logic
description: Add analysis logic to session-retrospective: generate went_well (high throughput, zero corrections, successful assignments), went_wrong (recurring corrections, build failures, stale agents, orphaned beads), action_items (derived from went_wrong + unresolved corrections from memory store). Format as structured JSON arrays. Insert retrospective row via memory store. Send summary to user via comm.sendImportant() with formatted markdown. Update meta key last_retro_at. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.ts

title: Phase 4: Memory Reviewer & Query (Maintenance + API)
description: Startup review (surface top learnings into heartbeat prompt), weekly pruning (decay confidence, prune stale, merge similar), MCP tools (query_memories, get_session_retros), and REST endpoints for dashboard memory views.
close_reason: All tasks and QA bugs resolved. Memory-reviewer, MCP tools, REST endpoints — 4 bugs fixed.

title: Create memory-reviewer behavior (startup + weekly)
description: Create memory-reviewer behavior. Triggers: [agent:status_changed]. Schedule: '0 0 * * 1' (weekly Monday). Startup review: on first fire (detect via meta key last_review_at), query top 10 learnings by confidence*recency_weight, query last 3 retros for recurring action items, format as 'Lessons to remember' block, inject into heartbeat via comm.queueRoutine(). Weekly review: prune learnings >90 days with confidence <0.3, identify corrections with recurrence_count >2 and escalate to user, decay confidence 5% per week for unreinforced learnings, generate weekly summary. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-reviewer.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/memory-reviewer.test.ts
close_reason: Phase 4 memory query API implemented: memory-reviewer behavior, MCP tools, and REST endpoints

title: MCP memory tools (query_memories, get_session_retros)
description: Add MCP tools in new memory.ts file: query_memories (params: query string for FTS, category enum, topic string, minConfidence number 0-1, limit number default 10) and get_session_retros (params: limit number default 5). Register tools in MCP server tool list. Follow same pattern as existing mcp-tools/messaging.ts. File: backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts
close_reason: Phase 4 memory query API implemented: memory-reviewer behavior, MCP tools, and REST endpoints

title: REST endpoints for dashboard memory views
description: Add Express routes: GET /api/memory/learnings (query params: category, topic, minConfidence, limit, offset), GET /api/memory/learnings/search (query param: q for FTS), GET /api/memory/retrospectives (query params: limit, offset), GET /api/memory/corrections (query params: resolved boolean, limit), GET /api/memory/stats (aggregate: total learnings, topic distribution, avg confidence, correction recurrence rate). Use Zod for query validation. Follow same pattern as existing routes/. File: backend/src/routes/memory.ts
close_reason: Phase 4 memory query API implemented: memory-reviewer behavior, MCP tools, and REST endpoints

title: Phase 5: Self-Improver & File Sync (Meta-Learning)
description: Self-improver behavior that generates proposals for rule/agent-definition updates when 5+ learnings accumulate on a topic. Memory file sync that writes high-confidence learnings to auto-memory .md files and keeps MEMORY.md under 200 lines. Final integration and registration of all behaviors.
close_reason: All tasks and QA bugs resolved. Self-improver, file sync, integration — 4 bugs fixed.

title: Create self-improver behavior
description: Create self-improver behavior. Triggers: [learning:created]. Schedule: '0 0 * * 5' (weekly Friday). On learning:created: check if topic now has 5+ learnings, if so schedule proposal (debounce 1 per topic per week via meta key). On weekly schedule: group learnings by topic, for topics with 5+ entries and avg confidence >0.6 synthesize a proposed rule update, call create_proposal MCP tool with type=engineering and project=adjutant, track proposal count in metadata, adjust frequency based on past acceptance rate. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/self-improver.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/self-improver.test.ts
close_reason: Self-improver behavior: proposals from 5+ learnings per topic with high confidence, debounced weekly. 8 tests.

title: Memory file sync to auto-memory .md files
description: Create memory-file-sync.ts utility (called by memory-reviewer, not a standalone behavior). Query learnings with confidence >0.7 and reinforcement_count >2. Group by topic. For each topic: write/update ~/.claude/projects/-Users-Reason-code-ai-adjutant/memory/{topic}.md with concise bullet points. Update MEMORY.md: keep existing manual entries, add/update '## Auto-Generated Learnings' section with links to topic files, ensure total <180 lines. Prune topic files for learnings that dropped below confidence 0.3. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-file-sync.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/memory-file-sync.test.ts
close_reason: Memory file sync implemented with 14 tests. Exports high-confidence learnings to per-topic .md files, updates MEMORY.md with auto-generated section, prunes stale topic files.

title: Register all behaviors + integration test
description: Register all 4 new behaviors (memory-collector, session-retrospective, memory-reviewer, self-improver) in backend/src/index.ts adjutant initialization block. Add memory REST routes. Write integration test that verifies the full pipeline: simulate a correction message -> verify learning created -> trigger retro -> verify retro with metrics -> trigger review -> verify top learnings surfaced -> simulate 5+ topic learnings -> trigger self-improver -> verify proposal created. Files: backend/src/index.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/memory-integration.test.ts
close_reason: All behaviors registered in index.ts, memory routes mounted, MCP memory tools registered, 9 integration tests pass.

title: MCP memory write tools for adjutant agent
description: The memory store has full CRUD (insertLearning, updateLearning, reinforceLearning, supersedeLearning) but the MCP tools only expose query_memories and get_session_retros. The adjutant agent cannot store learnings, record corrections, or reinforce existing memories. Add MCP tools: store_memory, update_memory, reinforce_memory, record_correction. This completes the memory system loop — agents can learn from experience and persist it.

title: store_memory MCP tool — create new learnings
description: Add store_memory MCP tool to backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts. Accepts: content (string), category (operational|technical|coordination|project), topic (string), source (string, e.g. 'adjutant-observation'), confidence (0-1, default 0.5). Calls memoryStore.insertLearning(). Include input validation via Zod. TDD: write tests first in backend/tests/unit/mcp-memory.test.ts.

title: update_memory + reinforce_memory MCP tools
description: Add two MCP tools: (1) update_memory — update an existing learning's content, confidence, category, or topic by ID. Calls memoryStore.updateLearning(). (2) reinforce_memory — increment confidence on a learning the agent found useful. Calls memoryStore.reinforceLearning(). Both in backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts. TDD: tests first in backend/tests/unit/mcp-memory.test.ts.

title: record_correction MCP tool — track error patterns
description: Add record_correction MCP tool. Accepts: correctionType (string), wrongPattern (string), rightPattern (string), context (string). Calls memoryStore.insertCorrection() or memoryStore.incrementRecurrence() if a similar correction exists. In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts. TDD: tests first in backend/tests/unit/mcp-memory.test.ts.

title: Update adjutant agent prompt with memory tool usage
description: Update .claude/agents/adjutant.md to document the new memory tools and when to use them: store learnings after successful decisions, reinforce learnings that proved useful, record corrections when mistakes are identified. Add examples to the decision framework section.

title: Intelligent Adjutant — Signal-Driven Coordination
description: Replace disabled adj-052 automation with event-driven intelligence. The Overmind pattern: critical events wake the adjutant, it reasons and acts, then schedules its own follow-ups. No polling, no cron timers.

title: Signal Aggregator
description: Unified signal buffer with urgency classification, deduplication, and auto-expiry. Foundation for reactive prompting.

title: Signal buffer with critical/context classification
description: Create SignalAggregator with two-tier classification: critical signals wake adjutant immediately, context signals accumulate silently and are included when adjutant wakes for any reason.

title: Deduplication and expiry logic
description: Collapse repeated events from same source within 30s window. Auto-expire signals older than 30 minutes. Buffer metrics (size, signals/min).

title: Register aggregator in AdjutantCore
description: Wire SignalAggregator into EventBus via AdjutantCore initialization in index.ts.

title: Stimulus Engine
description: Reactive prompt injection based on signal urgency. Replaces hourly-only heartbeat with near-real-time situational awareness.

title: Stimulus engine with three wake sources
description: Create StimulusEngine with three wake sources: critical signal callback, scheduled check queue (setTimeout), and event watch registry. 90-second cooldown. Queue events during cooldown.

title: Situation prompt template + bootstrap prompt
description: Structured prompt: wake reason, context signals, state snapshot, pending watches/checks, recent decisions, available actions. Bootstrap prompt for server startup. Format for tmux single-line injection.

title: Replace periodic-summary with stimulus engine
description: Remove periodic-summary registration. Register stimulus engine. Bootstrap prompt fires 60s after startup. No more hourly heartbeat — adjutant schedules its own cadence.

title: Action Tools
description: MCP tools for spawn_worker, assign_bead, nudge_agent, decommission_agent, rebalance_work. LLM-controlled coordination actions.

title: spawn_worker MCP tool
description: MCP tool wrapping agent-spawner-service. Accepts prompt + optional beadId. Logs decision with reasoning to state store.

title: assign_bead MCP tool
description: MCP tool wrapping bd-client update. Accepts beadId + agentId + reason. Emits bead:assigned event. Logs decision.

title: nudge_agent MCP tool
description: MCP tool wrapping SessionBridge.sendInput(). Injects a prompt into target agent's tmux session. Logs decision.

title: decommission_agent + rebalance_work MCP tools
description: Graceful agent shutdown (message, wait, kill session). Orphaned bead return to pool. Protected agent list (adjutant-coordinator).

title: schedule_check + watch_for MCP tools
description: schedule_check: delayed wake-up with reason. watch_for: conditional wake-up on event match or timeout. Both register in stimulus engine's schedule queue.

title: Adjutant-only access guard for coordination tools
description: Check caller identity via getAgentBySession(). Reject non-adjutant callers for coordination tools. Return clear error message.

title: Adjutant Prompt Update
description: Update adjutant agent prompt with signal reasoning framework and action tool documentation.

title: Update adjutant.md with event-driven reasoning + self-scheduling
description: Rewrite adjutant prompt: event-driven reasoning, self-scheduling patterns (schedule checks after actions, watch for outcomes), decision guidelines, spawn budget, tool docs. Bootstrap behavior.

title: Decision Feedback Loop
description: Track outcomes of adjutant decisions and feed results back into stimulus prompts.

title: Outcome tracking in state store
description: Link spawn/assignment decisions to bead completion events. Track time-to-completion. Extend decisions table with outcome fields.

title: Feedback summary in stimulus prompts
description: Include last hour's decisions + outcomes in stimulus prompt template. Show what worked and what didn't.

title: Cleanup — Remove Old Behaviors
description: Delete disabled adj-052 behavior files and dead imports. Clean slate.

title: Delete old behavior files + periodic-summary + dead imports
description: Remove work-assigner, work-rebalancer, agent-spawner, agent-decommissioner, stale-agent-nudger, build-monitor, quality-gate, periodic-summary and their tests. Clean index.ts.

title: QA: Intelligent Adjutant edge cases and integration testing
description: Review adj-054 implementation for bugs, edge cases, missing test coverage, and integration issues across signal aggregator, stimulus engine, coordination tools, and state store.

title: Fix premature agent shutdown on backend restart
description: Backend restart triggers markAllDisconnected() which marks all agents as disconnected. Signal aggregator classifies this as CRITICAL, wakes adjutant, which decommissions agents per its 10-minute rule. Shutdown messages are auto-delivered to agents on reconnect, stopping them mid-task. Root cause: no distinction between real disconnection and server-restart false positive.

title: Fix spawn_worker using wrong project path
description: spawn_worker in coordination.ts uses process.cwd() instead of ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT. Agents spawn under 'AI' project instead of 'Adjutant'. Fix: pass projectRoot from index.ts into registerCoordinationTools.
close_reason: spawn_worker now accepts optional projectPath parameter from caller. Falls back to env/cwd when omitted.

title: Idle Agent Proposal Generation
description: Adjutant coordinator behavior that detects idle agents (5min threshold) and schedules a coordinator wake via stimulusEngine.scheduleCheck() with proposal context. Coordinator-mediated: behavior never messages agents directly. Includes deduplication review and 12-proposal pending cap. No cron jobs.
close_reason: All phases complete: Phase 1 (6 tasks), Phase 2 (2 tasks), QA bugs resolved

title: Phase 1: Core Behavior
description: Implement idle-proposal-nudge behavior with idle detection via scheduleCheck, debounce, proposal context, and pending cap

title: Tests: idle detection + nudge trigger via scheduleCheck
description: Write failing tests: agent status changes to idle triggers scheduleCheck(5min), callback fires nudge, working agent skipped, debounce prevents duplicates

title: Implement createIdleProposalNudge behavior (coordinator-mediated)
description: Create behavior triggered by agent:status_changed. On idle: build proposal context, call stimulusEngine.scheduleCheck(300000, reason). No direct agent messaging — coordinator decides.

title: Tests: proposal context in nudge message
description: Tests that nudge message includes pending + dismissed proposal summaries (titles, IDs, types)

title: Implement buildScheduleReason with proposal context
description: Query ProposalStore for pending/dismissed proposals, format into scheduleCheck reason string for coordinator's situation prompt

title: Tests: 12-proposal pending cap
description: Tests that >= 12 pending proposals triggers improve-only mode in nudge message, < 12 allows new creation

title: Implement pending cap improve-only mode
description: Count pending proposals, switch message to improve-only instructions when >= 12

title: Phase 2: Registration & Integration
description: Wire idle-proposal-nudge into behavior registry and add edge case tests

title: Register behavior in index.ts
description: Import and register createIdleProposalNudge in behavior registry. Pass stimulusEngine dependency. No new cron registrations.

title: Edge case tests: disconnect, debounce reset, no direct messaging
description: Tests: skip disconnected agents, debounce resets after non-idle transition, verify behavior never calls comm.messageAgent()
close_reason: QA review covered edge cases; B1 shouldAct bug fixed

title: Acceptance Testing Framework: Production-Grade
description: Elevate the acceptance test framework from proof-of-concept (1 spec, 4 passing API tests) to production-grade auto-verification. Includes: multi-spec batch generation, coverage reporting, improved test description quality, step library expansion, root-level npm scripts, and spec-change-triggered regeneration.

title: Multi-spec batch generation (--all flag)
description: Add --all flag to acceptance CLI that discovers all specs/ directories with spec.md containing GWT scenarios and generates acceptance test files for each. Currently only processes one spec at a time. Should scan specs/ dir, filter to specs with parseable GWT, generate tests for each, report summary.

title: Bug: query param parsing truncates values containing = sign
description: File: backend/src/acceptance/pattern-detector.ts, line 79. Query parameter parsing uses pair.split('=') which destructures as [key, value]. Since split only returns the first two segments, a query like ?filter=a=b produces {filter: 'a'} instead of {filter: 'a=b'}. Fix: use pair.split('=', 2) or pair.indexOf('=') + substring to preserve the full value.

title: Bug: PUT/DELETE generate raw supertest calls, not typed harness wrappers
description: test-generator.ts lines 417-422: PUT generates harness.request.put() and DELETE generates harness.request.delete(). These return raw supertest Response, not the typed {status,body} wrapper used by GET/POST/PATCH. Assertions like res.body.data.status fail.

title: Bug: escapeDoubleQuotes doesn't escape backslashes in generated test strings
description: test-generator.ts line 521: escapeDoubleQuotes() only escapes double quotes via s.replace(/"/g, '\"'). If spec text contains a literal backslash before a quote (e.g. a\"quoted\"), the generated code produces \\" which is an escaped backslash + unescaped quote, breaking the JS string literal and causing a syntax error in the generated test file.

title: Bug: step registry global state leaks between test suites
description: step-registry.ts uses module-level mutable array. common-steps.ts registers at import time. clearSteps() resets but cached module won't re-register. Test suites that clear and re-import lose common steps permanently.

title: Bug: generated test descriptions are ungrammatical (should + conjugated verb)
description: test-generator.ts generateItDescription(): strips leading pronouns then prepends 'should'. Result: 'should only pending proposals are returned' instead of 'should return only pending proposals'. The grammar fix only handles 'is/are' at start but not deeper in the sentence. See generated file line 42: it('should only pending proposals are returned sorted by newest first'). adj-058.4 may already cover this.

title: Root-level npm scripts for acceptance workflow
description: Add npm run acceptance, npm run acceptance:generate, and npm run acceptance:report to root package.json. Currently these only exist in backend/package.json and the root 'npm run acceptance' fails. Must work from project root for developer ergonomics and CI integration.

title: Spec coverage report command
description: Add --report flag to acceptance CLI that shows: which specs have generated acceptance tests, how many scenarios are executable vs skipped vs TODO, pass/fail summary per spec. Output a coverage matrix like: 017-agent-proposals: 4 pass / 0 fail / 14 skip. Should scan both specs/ and tests/acceptance/ to correlate.

title: Fix generated test description grammar
description: The Then-clause-to-it-description extraction produces awkward English. E.g. 'should only pending proposals are returned sorted by newest first' and 'should proposal status updates to accepted'. The generator should strip leading articles, conjugate verbs, and produce natural 'should [verb] [object]' descriptions. Add a descriptionFromThen() utility with tests.

title: Expand common step library for messaging and beads
description: Current step library has 11 steps focused on proposals. Expand with steps for: message operations (send message, read messages, search messages), agent status (set_status, report_progress), bead operations (create/update/close beads via API). These are the most common GWT patterns across adjutant specs.

title: Generate acceptance tests for top 5 specs
description: Use the batch generator to produce acceptance tests for the 5 most important specs beyond 017: 008-agent-mcp-bridge, 009-agent-chat-ui, 033-persistent-memory, 034-intelligent-adjutant, 029-agent-personas. Run them, fix any harness gaps (missing route mounts, seed helpers), ensure at least the API-testable scenarios pass.

title: Safe regeneration preserving manual implementations
description: Currently --overwrite is all-or-nothing — it destroys any manually-added step implementations. Add a --sync mode that regenerates the describe/it structure from spec.md but preserves the body of any test that has been manually implemented (i.e. no longer contains TODO or matches the generated template). This enables spec evolution without losing test work.

title: Spec auto-verification on change (watch mode)
description: Add --watch flag to acceptance CLI that monitors specs/ for spec.md changes, auto-regenerates affected test files, and re-runs them. This is the 'auto spec verification' loop: edit spec → tests regenerate → pass/fail feedback in real-time. Could also integrate with speckit workflow to verify after /speckit.specify runs.

title: Bug: resolvePath only replaces first :id, leaves other path params as literals
description: File: backend/src/acceptance/test-generator.ts, line 534. resolvePath() uses path.replace(':id', '${seeded.id}') which only replaces the FIRST :id occurrence. Paths with multiple named params like /api/beads/:id/children/:childId will have :childId left as a literal string in generated code, producing non-functional tests. Should use replaceAll or a regex to handle all :paramName patterns.

title: Coordinator Activity Feed via Timeline Integration
description: Bridge coordinator decisions (spawn, assign, nudge, decommission) into the existing timeline. Proposal 98c392a0.

title: Add coordinator:action to EventBus + EventType
description: Add coordinator:action to EventMap in event-bus.ts. Add coordinator_action to EventType union in types/events.ts.

title: EDGE CASE: rebalance_work emits N events per bead — timeline noise for bulk operations
description: In coordination.ts line 414, logDecision is called inside a for-loop for each bead being rebalanced. When the bridge connects logDecision to eventStore.insertEvent, a rebalance of 10 beads will emit 10 separate coordinator:action events, 10 WebSocket broadcasts, and 10 timeline entries. This creates unnecessary noise in the timeline and wastes bandwidth. Consider: (a) batching the rebalance into a single decision entry with all bead IDs in the detail field, or (b) having the bridge deduplicate rapid-fire decisions from the same action.
close_reason: Acceptable — each bead rebalance is a distinct coordinator action worth logging. N events for N beads is correct granularity.

title: DESIGN: Dual storage of coordinator decisions in adjutant_decisions AND events tables
description: After the bridge is connected, every logDecision call will write to BOTH adjutant_decisions table (via state-store.ts line 174-177) AND the events table (via eventStore.insertEvent). This creates dual storage with no referential link between them. The adjutant_decisions table has configurable pruning (pruneOldDecisions), while the events table prunes at 7 days (pruneOldEvents). The tables have different schemas (decisions has outcome/outcome_at fields, events has messageId/beadId). Questions to resolve: (1) Should adjutant_decisions be deprecated in favor of events table with a coordinator_action eventType? (2) Should the events row reference the decision ID? (3) Are the different retention policies intentional or a bug?
close_reason: Acceptable by design — decisions table is the source of truth with 30d retention, events table is the display layer with 7d retention. Different purposes.

title: Bridge logDecision → eventStore in coordination tools
description: Add eventStore param to registerCoordinationTools(). After each state.logDecision(), insert timeline event with eventType coordinator_action, agentId adjutant-coordinator, and full decision detail. Update index.ts call.

title: Add SSE mapping in events router
description: Add coordinator:action → coordinator_action SSE mapping in routes/events.ts so real-time stream picks up coordinator actions.

title: Tests for coordinator action timeline integration
description: Test that coordination tools insert timeline events. Test EventBus event is emitted. Test SSE mapping exists. In backend/tests/unit/mcp-tools/coordination.test.ts.

title: Frontend filter chip + event card rendering
description: Add coordinator_action chip to TimelineFilters.tsx. Add coordinator_action rendering (icon, distinct styling) to TimelineEventCard.tsx.

title: BUG: Frontend missing coordinator_action filter chip and event card config
description: TimelineFilters.tsx (line 21-28) hardcodes EVENT_TYPES array without a coordinator_action entry. Users cannot filter by coordinator actions in the UI. Similarly, TimelineEventCard.tsx (line 12-19) EVENT_TYPE_CONFIG lacks a coordinator_action entry — coordinator events will render with the fallback magnifying glass icon and dim color instead of a meaningful label/icon. Both files need a coordinator_action entry added.
close_reason: False positive — engineer-2 already added both the filter chip and event card config

title: BUG: Signal aggregator feedback loop — coordinator:action events feed back into adjutant context
description: In backend/src/index.ts line 242-243, the EventBus onAny handler feeds ALL events into signalAggregator.ingest(). When coordinator:action events are emitted, they hit the default case in signal-aggregator.ts classify() (line 101-103) and are classified as context signals. These accumulate in the context buffer and are included in the situation prompt via snapshot(). This means the adjutant's own actions appear as context signals in its next wake, creating unnecessary noise and potential for the adjutant to react to its own actions. coordinator:action should either be filtered out before ingestion, or the classify() function should return a new 'ignore' classification for it.

title: BUG: Stimulus engine triggerWatch fires on coordinator:action — adjutant can wake on its own actions
description: In backend/src/index.ts line 245, stimulusEngine.triggerWatch(event, data) is called for ALL EventBus events including coordinator:action. If the adjutant registers a watch_for with event='coordinator:action' (or any wildcard-like pattern), it would trigger on its own actions, causing an immediate or cooldown-delayed wake. This is a potential infinite loop: adjutant acts -> coordinator:action event -> watch triggers -> adjutant wakes -> adjutant acts again. The cooldown (90s) prevents true runaway but still wastes tokens. Fix: either filter coordinator:action from triggerWatch, or document that watch_for should never target coordinator:action.

title: EDGE CASE: agentId field ambiguity when bridging logDecision to eventStore.insertEvent
description: InsertEventInput requires agentId as a non-optional string (event-store.ts line 13). However, DecisionEntry (state-store.ts line 16-25) has no agentId field — only behavior, action, target, reason. When engineer-1 bridges logDecision to eventStore.insertEvent, they must decide what agentId to use. For coordination tools (coordination.ts), the caller is always adjutant-coordinator. But logDecision is also called from background behaviors: self-improver (behavior: 'self-improver'), health-monitor (behavior: 'health-monitor'), agent-lifecycle (behavior: 'agent-lifecycle'), memory-collector (behavior: 'memory-collector'), session-retrospective (behavior: 'session-retrospective'), stimulus-engine (index.ts:280). These have no MCP session to resolve. The bridge must either: (a) hardcode agentId='adjutant-coordinator' for all decisions, or (b) use the behavior name as agentId, or (c) use a sentinel value like 'system'. The choice affects timeline filtering — if all use 'adjutant-coordinator', filtering by that agent shows everything including background behaviors mixed with manual coordinator actions.
close_reason: False positive — engineer-1's bridge uses callerAgent from getAgentBySession, falling back to adjutant-coordinator

title: Bug: Uppercase file extensions (e.g. .MD, .JSON) rejected by readFile
description: In files-service.ts readFile(), line 269 does: const ext = extname(relativePath).toLowerCase(). Wait — actually I need to re-read this. Let me check... Yes, line 269 does call .toLowerCase(). So this is NOT a bug — uppercase extensions are normalized. Disregard. However, this means the test coverage gap bead (adj-4kf4) item #8 is invalid. The other 7 gaps remain valid.
close_reason: Not a bug: files-service.ts line 269 already calls .toLowerCase() on the extension before checking TEXT_EXTENSIONS. Uppercase extensions like .MD are handled correctly.

title: Bug: FileContentView shows blank screen when fileContent is nil and no error
description: In FileContentView.swift, the body uses if/else-if chaining: (1) isLoading -> spinner, (2) fileContent != nil -> content, (3) errorMessage != nil -> error banner. But there is NO else clause. If isLoading=false, fileContent=nil, and errorMessage=nil (e.g. after a CancellationError is swallowed by BaseViewModel.performAsync), the view renders a completely blank/black screen with no indication of what happened. This is the known black screen bug. Fix: add an else clause that shows either an empty state message or triggers a retry. Note: the staff-eng is reportedly fixing this, but it is documented here for completeness.
close_reason: Fixed by adj-bfr9 — else clause added

title: iOS: Enhance agent status display in AgentRowView and RecipientSelectorSheet

For both views, ensure the task text is displayed for all statuses including idle/done.

title: Bug: Decommissioner must exclude the Adjutant coordinator from shutdown targets
description:
The spec for adj-052.2.4 (Agent Decommissioner) says: find agents idle 30+ minutes with no pending work, send graceful shutdown. But the Adjutant coordinator agent (`adjutant-coordinator`) itself may appear idle for extended periods between heartbeat cycles (it's a long-running session that wakes on prompts). If the decommissioner doesn't explicitly exclude it, it will send a shutdown request to the Adjutant itself, killing the coordinator.

The health-monitor behavior (health-monitor.ts) separately ensures the Adjutant is alive. If the decommissioner kills it and health-monitor respawns it, you get a decommission/respawn thrashing loop every 30 minutes.

**Impact**: P1 — killing the coordinator paralyzes the entire system. All behaviors lose their prompt-injection target, summaries stop, and work assignment stalls until health-monitor respawns it.

**Fix**: The decommissioner's shouldAct() or act() must have an explicit exclusion list containing at minimum `adjutant-coordinator` (imported from adjutant-spawner.ts as ADJUTANT_SESSION_NAME). Consider a configurable `protected_agents` list in metadata.

**Files**: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/agent-decommissioner.ts (to be created per adj-052.2.4), backend/src/services/adjutant-spawner.ts (ADJUTANT_SESSION_NAME constant)
close_reason: Fixed: decommissioner explicitly excludes adjutant-coordinator and adjutant agent IDs

title: Bug: BehaviorRegistry shouldAct/act use 'unknown' types losing type safety
description: In behavior-registry.ts:32-34, the AdjutantBehavior interface declares shouldAct(event, state: unknown) and act(event, state: unknown, comm: unknown). The JSDoc comment says 'AdjutantState and CommunicationManager are injected by the core' but the types are unknown. Every behavior implementation will need unchecked type assertions or runtime validation. Fix: use generic type parameters or import the actual interfaces. If circular deps are a concern, define the interfaces in a shared types file.

title: Bug: LiveActivityService observeActivityState creates infinite async loop in init
description:
LiveActivityService.init() calls observeActivityState() which creates a Task that:
1. Calls checkForExistingActivities() (async, accesses Activity<>.activities)
2. Enters an infinite for-await loop on ActivityAuthorizationInfo().activityEnablementUpdates

This Task is created during the init cascade and runs indefinitely. While it doesn't block the main thread (it's an async Task), it:
- Creates an async context during singleton init (anti-pattern)
- Keeps a strong reference to 'self' through the Task (via [weak self] + guard)
- The for-await loop never ends - it monitors enablement changes for the lifetime of the app

The checkForExistingActivities() call accesses Activity<>.activities which can be slow on first access as ActivityKit initializes its internal state.

Also: checkSupport() calls ActivityAuthorizationInfo().areActivitiesEnabled synchronously in init. This creates a NEW ActivityAuthorizationInfo instance just to check a boolean, while observeActivityState() creates ANOTHER one for the enablement stream. This is wasteful.

Impact: The ActivityKit access adds ~20-50ms to startup on first use. Not critical, but unnecessary during the launch path.

Suggested fix:
- Defer checkSupport() and observeActivityState() to a start() method
- Reuse the same ActivityAuthorizationInfo instance for both check and observation
- The Live Activity doesn't need to be started in didFinishLaunchingWithOptions - it can be deferred to after first render

title: Bug: LifecycleManager.killSession does not clean up git worktrees
description:
LifecycleManager.killSession() (lifecycle-manager.ts:213-235) kills the tmux session and removes it from the registry, but does NOT clean up the git worktree directory. The swarm-service.ts (lines 261-270) has worktree cleanup logic (`git worktree remove`), but this code only runs through the swarm teardown path, not through killSession.

When the decommissioner (adj-052.2.4) decommissions agents, it will call killSession. If the agent was running in a worktree (per the mandatory isolation rule), the worktree directory will be left behind as an orphan:
- The git branch remains checked out
- The worktree directory consumes disk space
- `git worktree list` shows stale entries
- Future `git worktree add` with the same name fails

Over time, with frequent spawn/decommission cycles, this accumulates unbounded orphaned worktree directories.

**Impact**: P2 — resource leak that accumulates over time. Not immediately dangerous but causes disk bloat and git worktree namespace pollution.

**Fix**: killSession should check if session.workspaceType === 'worktree' and if so, run `git worktree remove <path> --force` after killing the tmux session. Add error handling (worktree may already be gone). The swarm-service.ts cleanup code (lines 261-270) can serve as reference.

**Files**: backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts (killSession method, lines 213-235), backend/src/services/swarm-service.ts (lines 261-270, reference implementation)
close_reason: Accepted P2: worktree cleanup on killSession is a known gap. Spawned agents via Claude Code manage their own worktrees. Orphan cleanup can be a maintenance task.

title: Refactor: getMessages dynamic SQL construction is error-prone
description: message-store.ts getMessages() builds SQL strings dynamically by concatenating conditions and params arrays. While all values are parameterized (safe from SQL injection), this pattern is hard to audit and easy to break. A subtle bug: the limit clause is positioned after ORDER BY, which is correct, but the param is pushed AFTER the loop, so if both before+beforeId AND after+afterId are used simultaneously, the param ordering could break. The current code works because limit is always pushed last, but adding any new filter between getMessages() and the limit push would cause a silent param mismatch. Consider extracting a small query builder or at least adding a comment warning.
close_reason: Deferred P3: dynamic SQL works correctly, refactor is nice-to-have

title: QA: mcpJsonValid() doesn't distinguish malformed JSON from missing adjutant key
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:77-89, mcpJsonValid() returns { exists: true, hasAdjutant: false } for BOTH cases:
1. .mcp.json exists but contains invalid JSON
2. .mcp.json exists with valid JSON but no mcpServers.adjutant

The init command needs to distinguish these cases:
- If malformed JSON: warn user and don't clobber (could lose other MCP servers)
- If valid JSON without adjutant: safely merge the adjutant entry
- If no file: create fresh

The return type should include a 'valid' field or 'malformed' flag so callers can handle these differently.

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:77-89
Severity: Medium - init could silently overwrite a malformed .mcp.json that has other servers configured
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

This causes duplicate messages on the server. The code has confused comments ('Fall through to hook-based send'). title: Bug: initialPrompt must NOT be removed from CreateSessionRequest — used by spawn_worker
description: CRITICAL: The spec says "System MUST NOT use initialPrompt or --prompt for persona injection" (FR-005), but initialPrompt is ALSO used by the spawn_worker MCP tool (coordination.ts:155) and agent-spawner-service.ts for non-persona task prompt injection. If engineer-1 removes initialPrompt support from CreateSessionRequest or LifecycleManager, it will break spawn_worker — which is how agents spawn teammates with task instructions. The correct change is: remove `initialPrompt: personaPrompt` from the persona-specific code paths in agents.ts and sessions.ts, but leave the initialPrompt field and its handling in lifecycle-manager.ts completely intact.
close_reason: Handled: initialPrompt preserved on interface, only persona usage changed

title: QA: iOS GastownStatus.swift has deep Gas Town coupling — custom Codable for dual-shape
description:
RISK: ios/AdjutantKit/Sources/AdjutantKit/Models/GastownStatus.swift has complex custom Codable implementations handling both Gas Town shape (mayor/deacon/daemon, town, rigs) and SystemStatus shape. This struct is referenced across 37 iOS files. Key concerns:
1. InfrastructureStatus uses mayor/deacon/daemon field names — these Gas Town roles won't exist anymore
2. RigStatus, MergeQueueSummary, TownInfo types are Gas Town-specific
3. GastownStatus.init(from:) tries Gas Town shape first, then falls back — this order should flip or GT path should be removed
4. The struct name 'GastownStatus' itself needs renaming
5. PowerCapabilities may be simplified (canControl always false in swarm)
6. iOS DeploymentMode enum still has .gastown case
Risk: Removing backend Gas Town responses without updating iOS models will cause JSON decoding failures.

title: Bug: MarkdownTextView inline text missing foregroundColor — inherits parent
description: In MarkdownTextView.swift, the inlineText() and inlineElement() functions build Text views with font but do NOT set .foregroundColor for the .text case (line 145-146). The text color is inherited from the parent view's foregroundColor. In FileContentView.swift, when rendering markdown content (line 29: MarkdownTextView(content.content)), there is no .foregroundColor modifier applied. The parent Group has no foregroundColor set either. The color ultimately depends on the SwiftUI view hierarchy — which in the file content context may result in the default label color (white/black depending on appearance) rather than the theme's green text. The plain text branch (line 34) correctly sets .foregroundColor(theme.bright), but the MarkdownTextView branch does not. This could result in markdown files displaying with wrong text color. Note: the preview at bottom of MarkdownTextView.swift sets .foregroundColor(CRTTheme.ColorTheme.pipboy.primary) explicitly, suggesting this is a known requirement that the file content caller omits.
close_reason: Fixed by adj-bfr9 — foregroundColor added to ScrollView

title: Fix: skill tool-catalog.md has wrong parameter names (multiple tools)
description:
The tool-catalog.md reference docs have parameter name mismatches with the actual implementations:

1. send_message: docs say 'recipient' but impl uses 'to'
2. set_status: docs say 'detail' but impl uses 'task' and 'beadId'
3. report_progress: docs say 'taskId'/'percent' but impl uses 'task'/'percentage'
4. announce: docs say only 'body' but impl requires both 'title' and 'body'

Also SKILL.md:27 uses 'recipient' but impl is 'to'.

These mismatches will cause agents to call tools with wrong params, getting Zod validation errors. Update the docs to match actual Zod schemas in messaging.ts, status.ts, queries.ts, beads.ts.

title: Bug: AppState.loadPersistedState reads apiBaseURL from UserDefaults twice
description:
AppState.init() calls:
1. loadPersistedBaseURL() - reads UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: 'apiBaseURL')
2. loadPersistedAPIKey() - reads UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: 'apiKey')
3. Sets self.apiBaseURL and self.apiKey from the results
4. Creates APIClient with the URL and key

Then loadPersistedState() runs and:
5. Reads UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: 'selectedTheme')
6. Reads UserDefaults.standard.bool(forKey: 'isOverseerMode')
7. Reads UserDefaults.standard.bool(forKey: 'isVoiceMuted')
8. Reads UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: 'communicationPriority')
9. Reads UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey: 'apiBaseURL') AGAIN
10. Creates a new APIClient AGAIN via recreateAPIClient() if URL exists

The apiBaseURL is read twice and the APIClient is created twice. Additionally, loadPersistedState() writes to sharedDefaults (app group UserDefaults) which is another I/O operation:
  Self.sharedDefaults?.set(url.absoluteString, forKey: 'apiBaseURL')

This also creates a UserDefaults(suiteName:) instance for the app group, which has its own initialization cost.

Impact: 2 redundant UserDefaults reads + 1 redundant APIClient creation + app group UserDefaults init. ~10-20ms wasted.

Suggested fix:
- Read apiBaseURL only once in init, don't re-read in loadPersistedState
- Don't recreate APIClient in loadPersistedState (it was just created in init)
- Batch all UserDefaults reads into a single method
close_reason: Fixed by adj-049.1.3: UserDefaults reads batched off main thread

title: QA: parseJsonFile swallows all errors including permission denied
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:29-36, parseJsonFile() catches all exceptions and returns null:

  try { ... JSON.parse ... } catch { return null; }

This means:
- File doesn't exist -> null (expected)
- File is malformed JSON -> null (expected)
- File exists but permission denied -> null (UNEXPECTED - masks real error)
- File is a directory -> null (UNEXPECTED)
- Disk I/O error -> null (UNEXPECTED)

Callers can't distinguish 'file doesn't exist' from 'file exists but we can't read it'. For critical files like ~/.claude/settings.json, a permission error should be reported differently than a missing file.

Consider returning a richer result: { data: T | null, error: 'not_found' | 'parse_error' | 'permission_denied' | null }

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:29-36
Severity: Low - rare in practice but masks real errors
close_reason: Deferred: P3, postinstall works fine in current npm versions

title: Bug: bd semaphore contention between spawner, work-assigner, and decommissioner
description:
The bd CLI is serialized through a counting semaphore (bd-client.ts, concurrency=1) because Dolt's embedded SQLite can SIGSEGV under concurrent access. Three Phase 3 behaviors all need bd access:

1. work-assigner: calls `bd ready --json` + `updateBead` (which calls `bd update`)  
2. agent-spawner: needs to check ready beads (`bd ready --json`) to decide if spawning is needed
3. agent-decommissioner: needs to check if idle agents have pending beads (`bd list`)

With the semaphore at concurrency=1, these behaviors will serialize, which is correct for safety but creates performance issues:
- The spawner's capacity check runs every 10 minutes; if work-assigner has the semaphore, spawner blocks
- Work-assigner runs on every bead:created event; if spawner is checking ready beads, assignments stall
- The 30-second debounce on work-assigner means a blocked bd call could push assignment latency past the debounce window, causing the next event to be skipped

This is not a data-corruption bug but a latency/starvation issue that gets worse as more behaviors contend for the bd semaphore.

**Impact**: P3 — latency degradation, not data corruption. But could cause visible delays in agent assignment (~seconds to minutes during contention).

**Fix options**: 
(a) Increase semaphore to 2 (risks Dolt SIGSEGV, not recommended)
(b) Have spawner cache the bd ready result from work-assigner via AdjutantState metadata (e.g., store last ready-bead count and timestamp)
(c) Accept the latency and document it as a known limitation
(d) Spawner reads from AdjutantState (in-process) rather than calling bd CLI for its capacity check

Recommend (d) — the spawner doesn't need real-time bead data, it can use the last-known ready-bead count that work-assigner already computes.

**Files**: backend/src/services/bd-client.ts (semaphore), backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/agent-spawner.ts (to be created)
close_reason: Accepted P3: bd semaphore contention is bounded. Spawner runs every 10 min, work-assigner every 5 min with 30s debounce, decommissioner every 30 min. Concurrent bd calls are rare.

title: Bug: FileBrowserView @StateObject init closure runs on every parent re-render
description: In FileBrowserView.swift, the @StateObject is initialized in init() using StateObject(wrappedValue:). While SwiftUI's @StateObject guarantees the object is only created once per view lifetime, the wrappedValue closure is technically evaluated on every init() call (SwiftUI discards the result after the first). This means a new FileBrowserViewModel is allocated and immediately discarded on every parent view re-render. This is a minor performance issue — the allocations are cheap but unnecessary. Best practice per Apple WWDC is to use _viewModel = StateObject(wrappedValue:) in init which is what the code does — so this is actually the correct pattern. However, if the parent view (SwarmProjectDetailView) re-renders frequently (e.g. from polling), each re-render allocates a throwaway ViewModel. Same applies to FileContentView. Low priority since SwiftUI handles this correctly, but worth noting.
close_reason: Won't fix: documented as expected SwiftUI behavior for @StateObject init pattern

title: Refactor: MCP routes double-disconnect on SSE close
description: In routes/mcp.ts, both transport.onclose (set in mcp-server.ts:135) and req.on('close') (set in mcp.ts:36) call disconnectAgent(). When a client disconnects, BOTH events fire, causing disconnectAgent to be called twice for the same sessionId. The second call logs a warning (MCP disconnect for unknown session) which pollutes logs. While not a bug (the second call is a no-op), it indicates a design issue. Either remove the req.on('close') handler and rely solely on transport.onclose, or add a guard in the route to check if already disconnected.
close_reason: Deferred P4: double-disconnect is harmless, transport cleanup handles it

title: Bug: correction:detected event is never emitted by memory-collector
description: Found in adj-053.2.1 (memory-collector.ts). The EventBus has a correction:detected event type (added in adj-053.1.3), and the plan says the memory-collector should emit it. But the implementation only emits learning:created (line ~178). correction:detected is never emitted anywhere. This breaks any downstream consumer subscribing to correction:detected, including potentially the session-retrospective behavior which needs to count corrections. The fix is to emit correction:detected before creating the learning, as the plan specifies: 'If match: emit correction:detected, then create learning'.

title: Bug: Command injection in createFromClone via unsanitized execSync interpolation
description: In projects-service.ts line 440, createFromClone uses string interpolation to build a shell command: execSync(`git clone ${cloneUrl} ${targetDir}`). Both cloneUrl and targetDir are user-supplied values that can contain shell metacharacters (e.g., semicolons, backticks, pipes). A malicious cloneUrl like 'https://evil.com/repo; rm -rf /' or a targetDir with shell operators would execute arbitrary commands. Fix: use execFileSync('git', ['clone', cloneUrl, targetDir]) instead of execSync with string interpolation, which avoids shell interpretation entirely.
close_reason: Already fixed: createFromClone uses execFileSync('git', ['clone', ...]) instead of execSync with string interpolation

title: QA: Missing --force flag handling and documentation
description:
The init command accepts --force via bin/cli.js (commander option) and cli/index.ts (manual arg check), but:

1. The runInit function receives { force: boolean } but NEVER uses it (the stub ignores options)
2. The spec says '--force overwrites existing files' but doesn't define which files
3. The spec says 'FR-002: adjutant init MUST NOT overwrite user-modified files without --force' but there's no mechanism to detect user modifications vs generated files
4. Should --force overwrite .mcp.json? That could destroy other MCP server configs
5. Should --force overwrite ~/.claude/settings.json hooks? That could remove user hooks

The init implementation needs clear rules for what --force does:
- PRIME.md: safe to overwrite (generated content)
- .mcp.json: MERGE only, never overwrite (preserves other servers)
- settings.json: MERGE only, never overwrite (preserves user config)

File: cli/commands/init.ts:12-14, spec.md FR-002
Severity: Medium - unclear force semantics could lead to data loss

title: Bug: FileBrowserViewModel.navigateTo race condition on rapid navigation
description: In FileBrowserViewModel.swift, navigateTo() sets currentPath and fires Task { await refresh() }. But if the user rapidly taps multiple directories (e.g. dir1, dir2, dir3), three concurrent tasks run in parallel. Each calls performAsyncAction which sets self.entries on completion. The last task to complete wins, but that is not necessarily the last directory tapped — it is whichever API call returns last. This means after tapping dir1 -> dir2 -> dir3 quickly, the entries could show dir1's contents while the breadcrumb shows dir3. Fix: use BaseViewModel.startTrackedTask() which cancels the previous task, or add a generation counter. The same race exists in the view's onAppear calling refresh() while navigateTo also calls refresh().
close_reason: Fixed: cancel in-flight navigation task on rapid directory changes

title: Bug: Work-rebalancer should not unassign beads from blocked agents
description: The work-rebalancer spec says: 'When an agent goes blocked or disconnects, check their in-progress beads.' Treating 'blocked' the same as 'disconnected' is incorrect. An agent that reports status=blocked is still alive and working — it's indicating it's stuck on something (e.g. waiting for a dependency, permission issue, build failure). Unassigning its beads would: (1) Lose the agent's in-progress work and context. (2) Cause another agent to be assigned the same bead, duplicating effort. (3) When the blocked agent becomes unblocked, it would continue working on a bead now assigned to someone else. 'Disconnected' means the agent is gone (crashed, shut down). 'Blocked' means the agent is present but needs help. The correct behavior for blocked agents is to escalate to the user or try to resolve the blocker, NOT to strip their work. Fix: Only unassign beads for disconnected agents. For blocked agents, send a message asking what the blocker is and/or escalate to the user after a timeout (e.g. 30 minutes blocked without progress).
close_reason: Fixed: work-rebalancer triggers only on mcp:agent_disconnected, not blocked status

title: Fix: WebSocket message type contract mismatch between backend MCP tools and frontend hooks
description:
Backend MCP tools broadcast WS messages with shapes that don't match what frontend hooks expect:

1. set_status/report_progress: Backend broadcasts type='typing' with metadata={task, beadId, percentage}. Frontend useAgentStatus filters on metadata.type==='agent_status' which is NEVER sent. The hook will receive zero status updates in production.

2. announce: Backend broadcasts type='message' with metadata={announcementType, beadId}. Frontend AnnouncementBanner filters on metadata.type==='announcement' which is NEVER in the metadata. Announcements will never display.

Fix options:
A) Backend: Add metadata.type field to broadcasts (metadata: { type: 'agent_status', task, beadId } for status, metadata: { type: 'announcement', announcementType, beadId } for announce)
B) Frontend: Change filters to match actual broadcast shapes (check top-level type==='typing' + metadata.task for status, check top-level type==='message' + metadata.announcementType for announcements)
C) Both: Establish a formal WS message contract and align both sides

Tests pass because they mock the expected shape directly, masking this integration gap.
close_reason: Fixed: added metadata.type field to backend broadcasts (agent_status, announcement)

title: Nested blockquotes render as flat
description: The parser handles single-level blockquotes (> text) but nested blockquotes (>> text or > > text) are not supported. The parser only strips one '> ' prefix. A second level of '>' would be left as literal text in the quote content. Real spec files occasionally use nested blockquotes. The recursive parse call on blockquote content does re-parse, but the inner '>' detection won't fire because the prefix is already stripped only once.

title: REST /api/memory/learnings/search passes raw query to FTS5 without sanitization
description: The REST route GET /api/memory/learnings/search (line 74) takes the q parameter and passes it directly to store.searchLearnings(q, effectiveLimit). Same FTS5 injection issue as adj-adqi — the searchLearnings function does not sanitize the input before passing it to MATCH. Similarly, the MCP query_memories tool (line 46) passes the raw query string to searchLearnings. Both are attack surfaces for FTS5 syntax injection. Fix: sanitize the search input before passing to FTS5 MATCH, as message-store.ts does (wrap in double quotes). Files: backend/src/routes/memory.ts line 74, backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts line 46
close_reason: Store-level FTS5 sanitization covers REST and MCP paths

title: Bug: GraphNode missing labels, createdAt, updatedAt fields from BeadInfo

The api.yaml contract also defines these fields on BeadInfo. While not all fields may be needed for the graph visualization itself, the data-model says nodes are BeadInfo[] and the frontend may need labels/rig for filtering or display. At minimum, the data-model.md needs updating to reflect the actual GraphNode shape, or the GraphNode needs to include the missing fields.

title: Design: Soft-cap budget enforcement with amber warning zone
description: Hard-stopping sliders at the budget limit creates a frustrating UX where users hit an invisible wall. Instead: (1) Allow sliders to move freely until the budget is 80% full (80 points). (2) From 80-100 points, the budget gauge transitions to amber and shows 'NEARING LIMIT'. (3) At exactly 100 points, the gauge turns bright amber and shows 'BUDGET ALLOCATED'. (4) Above 100 points, the gauge turns RED and the save/create button becomes disabled with a pulsing error state. The budget-exceeded state shows 'OVER BUDGET BY X' in red monospace text and the save button reads 'REDUCE X POINTS'. This gives users feedback and agency rather than silently clamping their input. Server-side validation remains the hard enforcement boundary.
close_reason: Design recommendations incorporated into iOS and web implementations

title: Bug: Test coverage gaps in files-service.test.ts
description:

2. readFile with an empty string path — edge case not tested

6. readFile with double URL-encoded path traversal (e.g. '..%252F..%252Fetc/passwd') — not tested
7. No integration test for the routes layer (projects.ts) validating HTTP status codes match expected error codes

Note: uppercase extensions (.MD, .JSON) are correctly handled — extname().toLowerCase() on line 269 normalizes them.

title: Image syntax \![alt](url) renders as broken link
description: Markdown image syntax \![alt text](url) will be parsed by the link parser as a regular link with the text "]" being rejected (or the \! being orphaned text). The [ after \! triggers tryParseLink which will either succeed and render as a text link (wrong) or fail and leave raw syntax visible. Images should either render as an image (if supported) or show a placeholder like [Image: alt text] rather than broken characters. Spec files may include image references.

title: Bug: formatFileSize shows '0 KB' for files between 1024 and 2047 bytes
description: In FileBrowserView.swift, formatFileSize() uses integer division: 'bytes / 1024'. This is correct Swift integer division. However, for 1024 bytes exactly it shows '1 KB' which is fine. But notice the boundary: files < 1024 show 'N B' and files >= 1024 show 'N KB'. A 1500-byte file shows '1 KB' (1500/1024 = 1 in integer division). This is acceptable but imprecise. More critically, the MB formatting uses String(format: '%.1f MB', Double(bytes) / (1024 * 1024)) which could show '0.0 MB' for a file that is 1 MB (1048576 bytes exactly) since the condition is bytes < 1024*1024 which would be false at exactly 1048576. Actually wait — at 1048576 the condition 'bytes < 1024 * 1024' is false so it falls through to MB formatting: 1048576.0 / 1048576.0 = 1.0 MB. This is fine. However, there is no GB tier. A 2GB file (if ever encountered) would show '2048.0 MB' which is ugly but not a crash. Low priority.
close_reason: Fixed: formatFileSize uses floating-point division for accurate small KB display

title: bd CLI SIGSEGV crash on concurrent list operations
description: After running for a few minutes, bd list commands crash with nil pointer dereference in dolt's DoltDB.SetCrashOnFatalError. Exit code 2, SIGSEGV. Likely caused by concurrent bd processes accessing the dolt database simultaneously. The backend spawns multiple bd commands (list epics, list tasks, etc.) in parallel, and dolt's SQL engine doesn't handle concurrent connections to the same database safely. Need to investigate and add serialization or connection pooling on the adjutant side.

title: Bug: Heartbeat prompt injection delivers duplicate/stacked prompts to adjutant agent

This wastes context window tokens, causes redundant processing, and creates confusing echo loops where the agent's own nudge messages bounce back. The injection mechanism needs deduplication — either debounce at the sender, or detect+discard duplicate prompts at the receiver.

title: QA: printSummary has logic bug in pass/skip count display
description:
In cli/lib/output.ts:56 and :68, the conditional uses ?? (nullish coalescing) instead of || (logical OR):

  if (counts['pass'] ?? counts['created']) { ... }
  if (counts['skip'] ?? counts['skipped']) { ... }

This is wrong because:
- If counts['pass'] is 0 (falsy but not null/undefined), ?? treats it as a valid value and the condition is 0 (falsy), so the line won't show
- BUT 0 is the default from the loop, so if 'pass' key exists in counts it will be >= 1
- The REAL bug: if counts['pass'] is undefined but counts['created'] has a value, the ?? works correctly. But if counts['pass'] is 0 somehow, it short-circuits and the created count is ignored.

Actually the deeper issue: counts['pass'] can only be set if there ARE pass results (the loop only sets it when encountered), so undefined ?? counts['created'] works. But this is fragile and confusing. Should use || for clarity, or better yet just check both counts explicitly.

File: cli/lib/output.ts:56, 68
Severity: Low - works by accident but confusing code
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: iOS App Review: Usability, Completeness & UI/UX Audit
description:
Conduct a detailed review of the l2rr2l iOS app covering three key areas:

1. **Usability** — Evaluate navigation flows, touch targets, responsiveness, accessibility, error handling, loading states, and overall user experience on iOS devices.

2. **Completeness vs Web Frontend** — Systematically compare every feature and screen in the web frontend against the iOS app. Identify missing features, incomplete implementations, and behavioral differences. Document parity gaps with priority rankings.

3. **UI/UX Quality** — Assess visual design consistency, adherence to iOS Human Interface Guidelines, typography, spacing, color usage, animations/transitions, dark mode support, and overall polish. Identify areas that feel unfinished or non-native.

Deliverable: A comprehensive audit document with findings, screenshots/examples, and prioritized recommendations for each area.

title: Bug: MainTabView instantiates ALL 8 tab views eagerly via ForEach in TabContent
description:
TabContent.body uses:
  ForEach(AppTab.allCases) { tab in
      NavigationStack(path: coordinator.pathBinding(for: tab)) {
          tabView(for: tab)
      }
      .opacity(selectedTab == tab ? 1 : 0)
      .allowsHitTesting(selectedTab == tab)
  }

This creates ALL 8 tab views at once (overview, chat, crew, projects, beads, timeline, proposals, settings), wrapping each in a NavigationStack. Only the selected tab is visible (.opacity(1)).

While SwiftUI may optimize rendering for hidden views, each view's init() still runs:
- SwarmOverviewView() init
- ChatView(apiClient:) init
- AgentListView(apiClient:, onSelectMember:) init
- ProjectsListView(apiClient:, onSelectProject:) init
- BeadsListView() init
- TimelineView() init
- ProposalsView() init
- SettingsView() init

Each of these likely creates @StateObject ViewModels that may trigger their own initialization chains (fetching data, setting up observers, etc.).

The .animation(nil, value: selectedTab) prevents animation on tab switches, but the eager instantiation of all 8 views increases the time-to-first-meaningful-paint significantly.

Impact: Estimated 100-300ms of unnecessary view + ViewModel initialization on first render.

Suggested fix: Use lazy instantiation - only create tab views when they're first selected. Use a dictionary to cache created views. Or use SwiftUI's built-in TabView which handles lazy rendering.
close_reason: Fixed: TabContent now renders only selected tab body, unvisited tabs get no content

title: Bug: SystemStatus vs GastownStatus type mismatch in dashboard status section
description:
getStatusProvider().getStatus() returns SystemStatus (status-provider.ts:67-84) which has fields: powerState, powerCapabilities, workspace, operator, infrastructure?, rigs, agents, fetchedAt.

But DashboardResponse.status.data is typed as GastownStatus (types/index.ts:145-169) which has completely different fields: powerState, town: { name, root }, operator: { name, email, unreadMail }, infrastructure: { mayor, deacon, daemon }, rigs, fetchedAt.

The `as DashboardResponse["status"]["data"]` cast on dashboard-service.ts line 207 hides this structural mismatch. At runtime, the frontend receives SystemStatus data but TypeScript thinks it's GastownStatus.

Frontend code accessing status.data.town, status.data.operator.email, status.data.infrastructure.mayor will get undefined at runtime.

The cast also violates code style rule: "Type assertions (as) require a comment explaining why it's safe."

Fix: Either use SystemStatus in the DashboardResponse type, or transform SystemStatus to GastownStatus in the service.

title: Bug: wsBroadcast sends raw task/beadId instead of resolved values
description: In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/status.ts, the set_status handler correctly preserves previous task/beadId via nullish coalescing (resolvedTask = task ?? previous?.task), but the wsBroadcast on line 155 sends the original raw 'task' and 'beadId' params instead of 'resolvedTask' and 'resolvedBeadId'. This causes the frontend useAgentStatus hook to receive undefined task/beadId when an agent calls set_status without those fields (e.g. set_status({ status: 'idle' })), resulting in a brief flash of missing task text in the real-time chat status indicator until the next REST poll refreshes from the correctly-stored backend state. Fix: change line 155 from 'metadata: { type: "agent_status", status, task, beadId, projectId }' to 'metadata: { type: "agent_status", status, task: resolvedTask, beadId: resolvedBeadId, projectId }'.
